title: Death And rebirth
wikitext:
[]: Death And rebirth             Quote Post by cloud7900 » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:20 pm What new scenes are in the movie Death And Rebirth exactly?????i just saw it, and the only new scenes i found where: Kaji and azuka in the ship at night several times trought the movie the pilots playing classical instuments (cello, etc.) the scene where Shinji tells azuka of Kaji's dead. what other scenes are there? Is mi version of the movie incomplete?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:36 pm Not that I'm aware of, although I've only watched it twice, I don't think I've noticed anything other than what you've brought up.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:40 pm Your version is actually overcomplete. To view it as Anno feels it should optimally be seen, you need Death (True)^2 , which actually doesn't have those "new" scenes. Those scenes aren't exclusive to the movie, BTW. They're found in the DC versions of the television episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:46 pm CorporalChaos wrote: Not that I'm aware of, although I've only watched it twice, I don't think I've noticed anything other than what you've brought up.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:49 pm Shin-seiki wrote: There are several of the "Directors Cut" scenes in D&R (most of the 2I stuff at the beginning (also in #21'), Asuka in the bath (#22'), Yui talking with Fuyutsuki by the lake (#21'), Asuka finding out from Shinji that Kaji is dead (#24'), and Asuka and Kaji on OTRB (#22') The only stuff that is entirely unique to Death, IIRC, are the Recital scenes.

[]:             Quote Post by cloud7900 » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:49 pm are DC versions for all episodes or just 21-24?

[]:             Quote Post by cloud7900 » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:51 pm and also, in those DC episodes, there are more new scenes? (much or few scenes?)

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:51 pm cloud7900 wrote: are DC versions for all episodes or just 21-24?

[]:             Quote Post by cloud7900 » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:53 pm then what am i downloading on my utorrent right now????? (remastered episodes 1-26, remastered movies)

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:57 pm cloud7900 wrote: then what am i downloading on my utorrent right now????? (remastered episodes 1-26, remastered movies)

[]:             Quote Post by cloud7900 » Sun Sep 09, 2007 9:59 pm okz what do you mean by IIRC? (pardon my ignorance)

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 09, 2007 10:02 pm cloud7900 wrote: okz what do you mean by IIRC? (pardon my ignorance)

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Sep 09, 2007 10:08 pm The DC added scenes (please keep in mind that those scenes were never cut out of anything, they were added to the Home Video version, well after the series' airing) are absolutely indespensible, and the DC eps (21'-24') are the final 'canon' version of those eps.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Sep 09, 2007 10:53 pm During Rei I's death, there's a reflection of Kaworu in her eyes, then a red background sketch of Kaworu that appears. That's unique to Death. Wasn't the platinum release based off of Renewal in Japan? Gainax did the remastering, redid the audio, etc... and ADV just took it and redid their English dub, or am I dreaming again?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Sep 09, 2007 10:59 pm Ornette wrote: Wasn't the platinum release based off of Renewal in Japan? Gainax did the remastering, redid the audio, etc... and ADV just took it and redid their English dub, or am I dreaming again?

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sun Sep 09, 2007 11:03 pm Ornette wrote: During Rei I's death, there's a reflection of Kaworu in her eyes, then a red background sketch of Kaworu that appears. That's unique to Death. Wasn't the platinum release based off of Renewal in Japan? Gainax did the remastering, redid the audio, etc... and ADV just took it and redid their English dub, or am I dreaming again?

[]:             Quote Post by perfected_sound » Mon Sep 10, 2007 11:53 am I heard the recital scenes are meant to be taken as a factual event? As in, not some kind of AU moment?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Sep 10, 2007 12:56 pm The DC scenes in Death are only shown in the original version of Death (which was only shown in theaters). Death(true) had some....thing added or deleted to it as well, I can't recall what, while Death(true^2) is the only version released in the States as well as with Renewal. The recital scenes ARE AU, seeing it was impossible for Shinji and the others to all be there together based on what happened to them in the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Mon Sep 10, 2007 3:15 pm The recital scenes are more like a metaphor for the narrative of Death; especially considering how the next character to enter the room is the main focus for the next 15 minutes or so.

[]:             Quote Post by Sandoz » Mon Sep 10, 2007 5:26 pm Shin-seiki wrote: They redid the subs (vastly improved, btw), and had to dub new dialog for the new DC scenes (well, actually, they already did those for the Directors Cut DVDs). Other than that, it sill the same old, lamentable dub from Perfect Collection, AFAIK...
